mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding aggregate wage income and consumption expenditure, which drives corporate revenue expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of all months.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 4.5% signals restrictive monetary policy that elevates the discount rate on future corporate cash flows and tightens financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or Rank IC is positive over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent contraction in industrial production and acceleration in consumer price inflation compress corporate profit margins and force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month percent change is negative and CPIAUCSL month-over-month change exceeds 0.2%
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of observations meeting the trigger condition over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days exclusively when the unemployment rate starts from an elevated level above 5.5%, reflecting labor-market-recovery dynamics that boost cyclical earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances when UNRATE is above 5.5% and declines from the prior month.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in retail sales signals strengthening consumer demand and lifts expected corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of months with positive RSAFS growth, or Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in housing starts reflect durable forward-looking residential investment and construction activity that raises aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A declining federal funds rate combined with accelerating payroll growth signals a Goldilocks monetary easing cycle where policy support intersects with resilient labor income, generating strongly positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month change is negative and PAYEMS month-over-month change exceeds 150 thousand
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 60% of triggered instances over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate spike exceeding 4.5% accompanied by a month-over-month increase of at least 0.2 percentage points triggers rapid risk-off deleveraging as recession probability rises, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 4.5% and UNRATE rises at least 0.2pp from prior month
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or the strategy Sharpe ratio is negative over a 3-year rolling window.
